old_value: {"id":"project-LICHEN-2auf.27","title":"Register Rust nodes with simulator before TX","description":"The heterogeneous fleet Rust node uses lichen_embassy::sim::SimRadio::connect, which opens TCP but does not send the required simulator REGISTER message. The server rejects the first TX (0x10), causing zero Rust TX/RX and false interop failure. Add registered connection support with sim_id, node_id, and position, then verify Rust/Python fleet exchange.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-15T14:07:43.216233Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-15T14:07:43.216799Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"project-LICHEN-2auf.27","depends_on_id":"project-LICHEN-2auf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-07-15T14:07:43.216798Z"}]}

new_value: Added SimRadio REGISTER/OK handshake with an independent TCP framing test; hetero-node registers with sim/node IDs and position before TX; fleet script builds checked-in source to a pinned target, passes x-position, waits for Rust failures, and fails on zero Rust TX or zero fleet RX. Verified 2 Embassy sim tests, hetero-node cargo check, focused clippy, bash -n, local Python simulator registration/TX smoke, git diff --check, and three final reviews.
